en.extract:
The Timor green pigeon (Treron psittaceus) is a species of bird in the family Columbidae. It is found on the islands of Rote and Timor. Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests. It is threatened by habitat loss.


Distribution and habitat
The Timor green pigeon is found on the islands of Rote, Timor, Semau, and Atauro in the Lesser Sunda Islands. It inhabits primary forest, monsoon forest, and secondary forest with tall trees in lowlands, occurring up to altitudes of 160 m (520 ft).


Behaviour and ecology
The Timor green pigeon is frugivorous and has been recorded feeding on figs.


References


External links

The '''Timor green pigeon''' ('''''Treron psittaceus''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Columbidae]]. It is found on the islands of [[Rote Island|Rote]] and [[Timor]]. Its natural [[habitat]] is subtropical or tropical moist lowland [[forest]]s. It is threatened by [[habitat loss]].

== Distribution and habitat ==
The Timor green pigeon is found on the islands of [[Rote Island|Rote]], [[Timor]], [[Semau]], and [[Atauro]] in the [[Lesser Sunda Islands]]. It inhabits [[primary forest]], [[monsoon forest]], and [[secondary forest]] with tall trees in lowlands, occurring up to altitudes of {{Convert|160|m|ft|abbr=on}}.<ref name=":0">{{Citation|last=Baptista|first=Luis F.|title=Timor Green-Pigeon (Treron psittaceus)|date=2020-03-04|url=https://birdsoftheworld.org/bow/species/timgrp1/1.0/introduction|work=Birds of the World|editor-last=Billerman|editor-first=Shawn M.|publisher=Cornell Lab of Ornithology|language=en|doi=10.2173/bow.timgrp1.01|access-date=2021-12-10|last2=Trail|first2=Pepper W.|last3=Horblit|first3=H.M.|last4=de Juana|first4=Eduardo|last5=Garcia|first5=Ernest|editor2-last=Keeney|editor2-first=Brooke K.|editor3-last=Rodewald|editor3-first=Paul G.|editor4-last=Schulenberg|editor4-first=Thomas S.|url-access=subscription}}</ref>

== Behaviour and ecology ==
The Timor green pigeon is [[frugivorous]] and has been recorded feeding on figs.<ref name=":0" />
